display_name: OSPF neighbor in 2WAY state on a multi-access segment is normal, not stuck
context: show ip ospf neighbor output on a broadcast or NBMA segment with more than two routers
observation: One or more OSPF neighbors show state 2WAY/DROTHER instead of FULL
naive_interpretation: The OSPF adjacency is stuck or failing to reach FULL state — there is a problem
correct_interpretation: On broadcast and NBMA network types, only the DR and BDR form FULL adjacencies with all neighbors. Non-DR/BDR routers (DROTHERs) remain in 2WAY state with each other by design. This is normal OSPF behavior per RFC 2328. Only worry if a neighbor that should be FULL (DR or BDR relationship) is stuck in a lower state.
why: RFC 2328 Section 10.4 specifies that adjacencies are only formed with the DR and BDR on multi-access segments
2WAY state means bidirectional hello exchange is working — the routers see each other, they just are not exchanging LSAs directly
Forming full adjacencies between all N routers on a segment would create O(N^2) adjacencies, which does not scale
DROTHERs receive their LSA updates via the DR, so they do not need direct FULL adjacencies with each other

confirmation_commands.purpose: Check the DR/BDR role column — 2WAY is expected between DROTHERs
Verify the network type (broadcast vs point-to-point) and this router's DR/BDR role
Check network type and DR/BDR election results

traps: Troubleshooting a 2WAY adjacency on a broadcast segment as if it were stuck
Trying to force FULL adjacency between DROTHERs by changing OSPF priority or network type without understanding the implications
Not checking the interface network type — point-to-point should always be FULL, broadcast is expected to have 2WAY between DROTHERs
Confusing 2WAY (normal on broadcast) with EXSTART/EXCHANGE (actually stuck — usually an MTU mismatch)
